Title: Overexpression of OsTF1L, a rice HD-Zip transcription factor, promotes lignin biosynthesis and stomatal closure that improves drought tolerance

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: shoot epidermis; inflorescence stem epidermis; leaf sheath sclerenchyma tissues and vasculature
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive downstream target of OsTF1L associated with increased lignification and drought tolerance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DHHC4 was identified by ChIP-seq/RNA-seq overlap as a direct OsTF1L target. 2. Its promoter was bound by OsTF1L and activated in transactivation assays. 3. The gene is discussed as a lignification-related factor linked to drought tolerance rather than as a directly metabolically characterized enzyme in this study.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; transient dual-luciferase transactivation assay; expression analysis in OsTF1L overexpression/RNAi plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in OsTF1L-overexpressing shoots; reduced in OsTF1L-RNAi shoots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought stress; constitutive OsTF1L overexpression or RNAi
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional role in lignification is inferred from prior studies on DHHC family proteins; this paper does not directly mutate DHHC4 itself.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: shoot epidermis; inflorescence stem epidermis; leaf sheath sclerenchyma tissues and vasculature
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive downstream target of OsTF1L associated with lignin deposition and drought tolerance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CASPL5B1 was one of five direct targets defined by integrated ChIP-seq and RNA-seq. 2. OsTF1L bound and activated its promoter. 3. The gene is implicated in lignin deposition/Casparian strip-type machinery based on homology and discussion, not direct standalone gene functional tests in this paper.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; transient dual-luciferase transactivation assay; expression analysis in OsTF1L overexpression/RNAi plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in OsTF1L-overexpressing shoots; reduced in OsTF1L-RNAi shoots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought stress; constitutive OsTF1L overexpression or RNAi
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Potential role inferred from CASP family function in recruiting lignin polymerization machinery.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: shoot epidermis; inflorescence stem epidermis; leaf sheath sclerenchyma tissues and vasculature
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive downstream target of OsTF1L associated with lignification and drought tolerance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Nodulin protein was identified as a direct OsTF1L target from ChIP-seq/RNA-seq intersection. 2. OsTF1L bound and activated its promoter. 3. The paper links it to lignification by analogy to Arabidopsis WAT1 homolog function.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; transient dual-luciferase transactivation assay; expression analysis in OsTF1L overexpression/RNAi plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in OsTF1L-overexpressing shoots; reduced in OsTF1L-RNAi shoots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought stress; constitutive OsTF1L overexpression or RNAi
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its lignification role is inferred from WAT1 homolog literature rather than directly dissected here.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive downstream target of OsTF1L associated with enhanced drought tolerance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAA-type ATPase was identified as a direct OsTF1L target. 2. OsTF1L directly bound and activated its promoter. 3. The gene is associated with drought tolerance rather than a defined nutrient metabolic terminal product in this paper.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; transient dual-luciferase transactivation assay; expression analysis in OsTF1L overexpression/RNAi plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in OsTF1L-overexpressing shoots; reduced in OsTF1L-RNAi shoots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought stress; constitutive OsTF1L overexpression or RNAi
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported in discussion as drought-tolerance related based on prior SKD1 literature; not a direct metabolic enzyme/regulator for nutrient accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative polymerization of lignin monomers during the final lignin-forming step in the cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: shoots, especially epidermis of inflorescence stem, leaf sheath sclerenchyma tissues and vasculature
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall stiffening and contribution to drought tolerance via improved water retention and tissue robustness
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation downstream of OsTF1L promotes lignin accumulation associated with drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. poxN/PRX38 was one of five direct OsTF1L targets identified by ChIP-seq/RNA-seq integration. 2. Its promoter was directly bound by OsTF1L and activated in transient assays. 3. The paper states poxN/PRX38 is responsible for the last step of lignin biosynthesis, linking OsTF1L directly to increased lignin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; transient dual-luciferase transactivation assay; expression analysis in OsTF1L overexpression/RNAi plants; lignin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in OsTF1L-overexpressing shoots; reduced in OsTF1L-RNAi shoots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought stress; constitutive OsTF1L overexpression or RNAi
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Serves as the clearest direct metabolism-relevant target connecting OsTF1L to lignin accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HD-Zip class IV transcription factor with HD, leucine zipper, START and SAD domains
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: shoots, particularly epidermis of inflorescence stem, leaf sheath sclerenchyma tissues and vasculature
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall stiffening associated with reduced water loss and improved drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: lignin biosynthesis and associated drought-responsive stomatal regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: poxN/PRX38; Nodulin protein; DHHC4; CASPL5B1; AAA-type ATPase
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates direct target genes and broadly upregulates lignin biosynthetic, stomatal movement and drought-inducible genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key regulator linking drought tolerance to enhanced shoot lignification and stomatal closure
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases lignin accumulation, enhances stomatal closure, reduces water loss, improves drought tolerance, and increases grain yield under field drought
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsTF1L overexpression improved drought tolerance at vegetative and reproductive stages without evident yield penalty, and increased grain yield under field drought. 2. OsTF1L overexpression increased shoot lignin accumulation whereas RNAi showed the opposite pattern. 3. OsTF1L directly bound and activated promoters of poxN/PRX38, Nodulin protein, DHHC4, CASPL5B1 and AAA-type ATPase. 4. Transcriptome data showed upregulation of drought-inducible, stomatal movement and lignin biosynthetic genes. 5. OsTF1L is mainly expressed in outer cell layers and vasculature, matching sites of enhanced lignification.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; RNAi knockdown; RNA-seq; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; transient dual-luciferase transactivation assay; qRT-PCR; lignin quantification; in situ hybridization; drought phenotyping; SEM stomatal assay; field yield analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all organs at multiple developmental stages; enriched in vasculature and outer cell layers including epidermis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in shoots, roots, leaves, node, stems, sheaths and flowers; induced transgenically in constitutive overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PGD1 promoter for overexpression (constitutive); GOS2 promoter for RNAi (constitutive); 35S promoter in protoplast transactivation assay (constitutive)
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No evidence of growth retardation or yield penalty in overexpression lines under tested conditions; RNAi reduced yield parameters
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to improve drought tolerance and maintain or increase grain yield under drought by combining lignification and stomatal closure traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although many downstream genes were differentially expressed, the study centers on OsTF1L as the decisive regulator and identifies only five direct targets by combined ChIP-seq/RNA-seq evidence.
